meta.title: Corder: Record what was said
meta.description: Corder records system audio and transcribes your meetings with Gemini 2.5 Flash. Speaker labels, searchable transcript, no bot in the call. Free to download for macOS 14+
meta.ogTitle: Corder. The Mac recorder with no bot in the call
meta.ogDescription: Record any meeting on macOS. Speakers labelled, transcript searchable. Google transcribes the audio and deletes it. No third participant ever joins your call

hero.headline: Record everything around you
hero.subhead: Keeps the {{Transcript}} on your Mac. No bot joins the call

audienceLine.text: For founders taking investor calls, consultants on client kickoffs, and anyone who thinks out loud and needs it written down
audienceLine._accentRangesNote: Word indices are 0-based, half-open [start, end). End is exclusive. AudienceLine.tsx reads these directly

privacy.heading: Your audio goes to Google. Then it is gone
privacy.subhead: Audio leaves your Mac once, for the transcription step. Google discards it when the call finishes. The transcript and the local audio file stay on your Mac until you delete them

cards.heading: Fast, accurate, free
cards.body: Audio uploads to Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash over HTTPS. Gemini transcribes it, returns a labelled transcript, and discards the audio. No retention. No training on your data. The endpoint is public: generativelanguage.googleapis.com

cards.heading: Your files, your Mac
cards.body: The transcript and the original audio live in ~/Library/Application Support/Corder/ until you delete them. Optional Dropbox archive uploads a copy to your own Dropbox app folder. It is off by default. Corder never uploads to any server other than Gemini for the transcription step

specList.value: Off by default, your Dropbox only
Remove the file from Finder, that is all

steps.heading: No bot in your call
steps.body: Corder records what your Mac plays and what your microphone hears at the OS level. The other side sees no third participant in the call

steps.heading: Studio-grade transcript, with playback
steps.body: Microphone and system audio go to Gemini as two parallel tracks, merged by timestamp. You get speaker columns and the original audio to scrub through later

steps.heading: Re-transcribe is free
steps.body: We cache raw turns by audio hash in a local SQLite. Coming back weeks later to re-map speakers or re-run the merge costs no extra API calls

steps.heading: No subscription. BYO Gemini key
steps.body: Bring your own Gemini API key. You pay Google about $0.30 per recorded hour at Flash rates, however many projects you're juggling

worksWith.heading: Records anything
worksWith.subhead: Corder captures audio at the OS level. It works with any app that plays sound. The list below is where most people start.
worksWith.footnote: Records anything that plays through your Mac, including Teams meetings where bot-based recorders are being blocked from mid-2026

cells.heading: Jump to any speaker
cells.body: Click any tick. The audio jumps there.

cells.heading: Records the screen too
cells.body: Video sits next to the transcript.

cells.heading: Catches the meeting first
cells.body: Menu bar widget, always one click away.

cells.heading: Drag out, no export dialog
cells.body: Drop into Notion, Apple Notes, anywhere.

cells.heading: No bot joins the call
cells.body: The other side sees one less tile.

cells.heading: Speaks your language
cells.body: Interface in yours. Transcription in forty plus.

pricing.heading: Pricing
pricing.subhead: Launch pricing on both plans, only for the next 3 months. The free tier always ships with the download

tiers.name: Monthly
tiers.billNote: Pay $24 upfront for 3 months. Launch offer, limited time
features: Priority support, direct line to the maker

tiers.name: Annual
tiers.billNote: Billed once a year. Just $8.25 a month
features: First in line for new features
Free upgrades to future Pro models

pricing.microcopy: Pricing in USD. No credit card to download. Free tier comes with the Mac app

items.q: Does Corder send my audio to the cloud?
items.a: For transcription, audio chunks are sent to the Google Gemini API and discarded after processing. Gemini does not train on this data per their API terms. The raw recording, the transcript, and the database stay only on your Mac. Speaker separation runs entirely on-device.

items.q: Does the other side know I am recording?
items.a: No. Corder records the audio that already plays through your Mac. No bot joins the meeting and no notification is sent to participants. You are responsible for telling them where local law requires it, for example California or Germany.
~/Library/Application Support/Corder/recordings/. You can browse, copy, or delete files directly in Finder. An optional Dropbox archive offloads originals to your own Dropbox if you set it up.

items.q: Does it record Zoom, Meet, Discord?
items.a: Yes, anything that plays through your Mac. Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, FaceTime, Discord, Slack huddles, browser-based calls, even Notion or Obsidian voice notes.

Transcription works in 40+ languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Italian, Polish, Dutch, Swedish, Czech, Turkish, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, Hindi, Arabic, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Ukrainian, and Russian. The interface is available in 20 of them.

items.q: Does it need an internet connection?
items.a: Recording works fully offline. Your meeting is saved locally regardless. Transcription requires internet for the Gemini API call. If you are offline when you stop a recording, Corder queues the meeting and transcribes it when the connection returns.

items.q: Which version of macOS does it require?
items.a: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later. macOS 15+ is recommended for the best performance.
Yes. Plain text, Markdown, JSON, or one ZIP with audio plus video plus transcript. Or drag any meeting straight into Notion, Apple Notes, or Obsidian and the formatting is preserved.

items.q: What is the difference between Flash and Pro models?
items.a: Flash (default in Free) is fast and accurate for everyday meetings. The Pro model on the Pro tier is slower but catches harder accents, technical jargon, and crosstalk better. Useful for sales calls, interviews, and technical syncs.

items.q: Is there a free trial for the Pro model?
items.a: Yes. The first 3 recordings on the Pro tier are free when you upgrade. Cancel before billing if it does not earn its keep.

fit.heading: Is Corder for you?
fit.subhead: Two cards. Read the one that sounds like you

yes.heading: Yes, if you:
items: Discuss technical work on those meetings
Don't want a third-party bot in your call
Often come back to what was said in past meetings

no.heading: Probably not, if you:
items: Want live captions while the call is happening
Need team-shared transcripts or workspace permissions

comparison.subhead: We don't name competitors on most of the page, but here's where Corder lands when you put us next to everyone

finalCta.lineUs: Free download. About $0.30 per recorded hour goes to Google
finalCta.footer: macOS 14+, Apple Silicon, Bring your own Gemini API key

footer.slogan: A meeting recorder that doesn't join the meeting

newsletter._note: Powers CorderPresenceForm (the morphed orb-to-card at the bottom of the page) and CorderPresenceStaticSection (reduced-motion inline fallback). The standalone Newsletter section was removed 2026-05-20 in favour of the morph chain. Same field shape, same copy.
newsletter.heading: Be the first to know
newsletter.subhead: New Feature Drops, No spam. We promise
newsletter.successMessage: Thanks. You'll hear from us when something ships

yoursPrivacy.heading: Your meetings stay yours
yoursPrivacy.subhead: Built different. Local-first, no cloud lock-in.

cards.heading: Local storage
cards.body: Recordings, transcripts, and your database live only on this Mac. Browse them in Finder anytime.

cards.heading: No third-party bot
cards.body: Corder records the audio playing through your speakers. The other side never sees a Corder bot in their call.

cards.heading: No telemetry
cards.body: No analytics, no usage tracking, no anonymous pings. The only network calls are Gemini for transcription and Sparkle for updates.

steps.heading: Record from anywhere
steps.body: One Dashboard. Hit Start from the menu bar, the global hotkey, or let Corder catch the meeting on its own. Sixty-six recordings, three hours, one keystroke away

steps.heading: Have your meeting
steps.body: Run your call on Zoom, Meet, Teams, Discord, anywhere. Corder records two parallel tracks, your microphone and everything that plays through your Mac. It works for as long as the meeting runs

steps.heading: Tune it to your workflow
steps.body: Toggle screen video, auto-transcribe, auto-title, the global hotkey. Switch theme or language with a click. Defaults you can change anytime, not preferences buried five menus deep
